author: parithosh
content: I've approved #11853, is it worth approving #11855 already though?  or are there more changes to #11855 worth waiting for?

author: jochembrouwer
content:
PR 11855 promotes the EIP from Draft to Review status and it is not recommended to do other changes on such status change PRs (I flagged this in 11853 which is why the status change got removed from there)
Approving it now will not merge the PR as the CI is failing: all referenced EIPs should at least have status Review (or better). 
This implicitly means that DFI section has to be removed (or all EIPs in DFI are at least in Review, which is something we cannot realistically expect from DFId EIPs for this fork)
Once approved it will merge once CI passes, so it would work the same as approving a "normal PR" (with a message like: LGTM, CI fails, please fix that and then we can merge)
Suggestion to approve if you agree with moving to Review status, this speeds up the process, once CI passes we can merge instead of waiting for author approval first 🙂
Thanks 😄

author: jochembrouwer
content: BTW, PR 11853 removes the CFI header, is this correct, are all EIPs in SFI?

author: jochembrouwer
content: I think it might be a mistake, have a PR here to re-add CFI and to restore EIP-8136/EIP-8189 stat https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11917

author: poojaranjan
content:
<@508125616940515329>  Appreciate the follow up. 

<@199561711278227457> appreciate the approval

> BTW, PR 11853 removes the CFI header, is this correct, are all EIPs in SFI?

Just to confirm, it was not a mistake. But, updated based on [Toni's comment](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11853#issuecomment-4924857405) and acknowledgement by ACDT team that they all are in devnet- 7.

I also mentioned in my [last comment](https://github.com/ethereum/EIPs/pull/11853#issuecomment-4953915393) 
> I do wish we’d been able to merge this a couple of weeks earlier, as it would have captured the transition from PFI to CFI. In its current form, all devnet EIPs have already progressed to SFI. Nevertheless, I hope the PR and commit history provide a clear record of those intermediate transitions.

For record, I like the sequential progress and not skipping any stage or status. Thanks <@508125616940515329> for updating the meta EIP-7773.

author: jochembrouwer
content:
Toni mentioned:

> I think all eips except 8282 and 7866 have been on a devnet and can be SFI'd? (one or two dfi'd i think). This could probably go with this pr.

Although most/all EIPs are now scheduled on a devnet I do not think this means that they are automatically SFId (EIP on a devnet is not, as far as I understand, the SFI status). I also do not see on the ACDT notes on forkcast or in the transcript (for the past 3 ACDTs, including todays ACDT) that most/all EIPs on devnet-7 are SFId: the scope of the devnet (which EIPs go in) is however finalized. Since this does not mean that the EIPs are SFId and there is also no mention to move those items to SFI, I do not think we can assume that this was meant (although from my side it looks fine to SFI all).

We should have put that on the agenda for today, but I think we can do this on Thursdays ACD also to explicitly move all these EIPs to SFI. What we should do now is to document for the future how these EIP stages for the fork are handled (when and where), such that this is an implicit agenda item of next ACDT meetings.

If I however have misunderstood the ACDT decisions or failed to read the transcript correctly then please prove me wrong, then we can immediately move those to SFI 🙂

I do not want to slow down the process, but I do think we should do the process carefully and not set a precedent where we do not follow the process, otherwise the confidence of the EIP stage in the Meta EIP would deteriorate, which is not what we would want.

What we should do now is document these kind of process events which do not go as expected in order to improve this in the future 🙂

When I'm wrong here please point it out, but to my understanding we have not explicitly marked these EIPs as SFI.
